name: Security Oversight Structure
lever_classification: governance — who oversees the security and surveillance system
consequences: Choosing a governance model for the surveillance system determines who can authorize monitoring actions and how accountability is enforced. A centralized board can streamline decision‑making but risks unchecked power, while a decentralized structure promotes transparency at the expense of slower responses. The oversight design will shape both operational efficiency and public trust.
options: Establish a joint oversight board composed of government officials, private investors, and independent civil society representatives to review security protocols quarterly.
Create a single, autonomous security agency staffed by former military personnel with a mandate to enforce surveillance without external oversight.
Deploy a blockchain‑based audit system that logs all surveillance actions in real time, accessible to all stakeholders for transparency.
review: Core tension: balancing control versus accountability. Weakness: options ignore potential legal challenges to surveillance data privacy and the cost of maintaining an autonomous agency.

name: Ecosystem Design Methodology
lever_classification: methodology — how to design the self‑sustaining ecosystems
consequences: The chosen methodology dictates how ecological zones are structured, affecting resource efficiency and system resilience. A modular approach isolates failures but adds complexity; a monolithic design simplifies integration but risks cascading breakdowns; a hybrid balances both. The design choice will determine long‑term sustainability and maintenance demands.
options: Adopt a modular bioreactor system that isolates each ecological zone, enabling targeted maintenance and rapid failure containment.
Implement a unified, interconnected ecosystem that shares resources across zones, maximizing efficiency but requiring robust redundancy.
Use a hybrid approach that combines modular bioreactors for critical functions with shared resource corridors for non‑critical services.
review: Core tension: efficiency versus resilience. Weakness: options overlook the impact of underground humidity on plant growth and the need for specialized ventilation.

name: Construction Sequencing Strategy
lever_classification: execution — how to sequence the construction phases across 144 floors
consequences: The sequence of building the 144 floors influences the project timeline, cost, and worker safety. A top‑down approach accelerates vertical integration but exposes workers to higher risk; a bottom‑up strategy reduces risk but delays upper‑level infrastructure; parallel construction can speed progress but requires tight coordination. The chosen sequence will shape resource allocation and risk management.
options: Begin construction from the lowest floor upward, completing foundational systems before adding residential and agricultural layers.
Start with the central core, installing power, water, and air systems, then build surrounding floors outward in concentric rings.
Employ a parallel construction strategy, simultaneously building multiple vertical shafts while coordinating inter‑floor utilities through modular prefabrication.
review: Core tension: speed versus safety. Weakness: options fail to address the logistical challenge of transporting heavy materials through narrow shaft access points.

name: Population Health & Resource Allocation
lever_classification: operations — how to manage population health and resource allocation
consequences: Operational protocols for health and resource distribution directly affect population well‑being and system stability. Strict rationing ensures long‑term sustainability but may lower morale; flexible allocation improves satisfaction but risks overconsumption. The chosen model will influence social cohesion and resource efficiency.
options: Institute a tiered rationing system that allocates resources based on age, health status, and occupational role, monitored by automated sensors.
Deploy a dynamic resource allocation platform that adjusts distribution in real time based on consumption data and predictive analytics.
Establish community‑managed resource committees that oversee distribution, fostering participation but requiring robust training and oversight.
review: Core tension: equity versus efficiency. Weakness: options ignore potential psychological effects of perceived inequity and the need for mental health support infrastructure.

name: Energy & Water System Integration
lever_classification: product — how to integrate advanced power generation and water recycling
consequences: The design of power generation and water recycling determines reliability, environmental impact, and long‑term operational costs. A single‑source renewable system reduces complexity but may be vulnerable; a diversified mix increases resilience but raises integration costs. The chosen product architecture will dictate maintenance cycles and sustainability.
options: Build a primary geothermal plant coupled with backup solar arrays to provide continuous power with minimal emissions.
Install a hybrid fuel cell system that uses biogas from agricultural waste, supplemented by micro‑hydro turbines within the underground water flow.
Deploy a modular battery storage network that stores excess renewable energy, paired with a closed‑loop water reclamation plant using advanced membrane filtration.
review: Core tension: reliability versus cost. Weakness: options overlook the impact of underground temperature fluctuations on system efficiency and maintenance cycles.

name: Stakeholder Communication Protocol
lever_classification: dissemination — how to communicate with external stakeholders and manage public perception
consequences: The dissemination strategy shapes transparency, stakeholder trust, and potential funding. High transparency builds confidence but may expose sensitive details; low transparency protects security but risks accusations of secrecy. The chosen approach will influence external support and public perception.
options: Publish quarterly progress reports with anonymized data, ensuring stakeholders receive updates while safeguarding critical information.
Host virtual town halls with key investors and government officials, providing live Q&A sessions to address concerns directly.
Release a curated media brief that highlights milestones and benefits, supplemented by a dedicated online portal for stakeholder inquiries.
review: Core tension: openness versus security. Weakness: options do not consider legal constraints on information disclosure and the potential for misinformation spread.

name: Silo Ventilation Calibration
consequences: Pulling this lever will adjust the pressure differential between underground levels, affecting air quality and structural integrity. A tighter calibration reduces energy consumption but may increase maintenance frequency for filters. Conversely, a looser calibration saves on filter replacements but risks higher CO2 buildup during peak occupancy.
options: Implement a dynamic pressure control system that adjusts airflow based on real‑time CO2 sensor data across all floors.
Adopt a fixed pressure differential strategy that maintains a constant airflow rate regardless of occupancy fluctuations.
Introduce a hybrid approach that uses pressure zoning, allowing high‑occupancy zones to have increased airflow while low‑occupancy zones conserve energy.
review: Balancing energy efficiency against air quality; the options ignore potential structural stress from pressure changes.

name: Population Morale Incentive Scheme
consequences: Pulling this lever will shape the reward structure that keeps residents engaged and compliant. A merit‑based incentive program boosts productivity but may create competition that undermines cooperation. A universal stipend approach reduces conflict but could dilute motivation for high performers.
options: Launch a tiered recognition program that awards residents for community service, with escalating benefits tied to participation levels.
Deploy a universal wellness stipend that provides all residents with monthly credits for health and recreation activities.
Establish a peer‑nomination system where residents vote for monthly morale champions, receiving symbolic tokens and public acknowledgment.
review: Balancing individual motivation against collective harmony; the options overlook long‑term sustainability of incentive funding.

name: Resource Allocation Hierarchy
consequences: Pulling this lever will define how critical resources like water, food, and power are distributed across the silo. A top‑down allocation model ensures rapid response to emergencies but may stifle local innovation. A decentralized model empowers floor managers but risks uneven resource distribution.
options: Create a centralized resource pool that reallocates supplies based on real‑time demand analytics from each floor.
Implement a floor‑level budgeting system where each level manages its own resource inventory and submits quarterly reports.
Introduce a hybrid allocation framework that reserves a core reserve for emergencies while allowing floors to trade surplus resources.
review: Balancing efficiency against autonomy; the options ignore potential for resource hoarding by floor managers.

name: Construction Material Sourcing Protocol
consequences: Pulling this lever will determine the procurement strategy for building materials. A single‑source supplier guarantees consistency but increases vulnerability to supply disruptions. A diversified supplier network spreads risk but may raise logistics complexity.
options: Negotiate long‑term contracts with a limited number of vetted suppliers to secure bulk discounts and quality guarantees.
Establish a multi‑source procurement plan that sources key materials from regional suppliers to reduce transportation distances.
Adopt a just‑in‑time delivery model that coordinates material arrivals with construction milestones to minimize on‑site storage.
review: Balancing cost control against supply resilience; the options fail to address potential regulatory constraints on material imports.

name: Security Protocol Hierarchy
consequences: Pulling this lever will set the layers of security controls across the silo. A strict hierarchical model centralizes decision‑making but may slow incident response. A distributed model empowers local teams but could lead to inconsistent enforcement.
options: Implement a tiered security clearance system where access rights are granted based on role and floor assignment.
Deploy a decentralized monitoring network that allows floor supervisors to override central protocols during emergencies.
Introduce a hybrid security framework that combines central command with local incident response teams.
review: Balancing control against agility; the options overlook the psychological impact of perceived surveillance on residents.

name: Governance Transparency Mechanism
consequences: Pulling this lever will decide how decision‑making information is shared with residents. Full transparency builds trust but may expose sensitive operational details. Limited disclosure protects security but risks eroding confidence.
options: Publish a monthly governance bulletin that outlines decisions, rationales, and upcoming policy changes accessible to all residents.
Establish a confidential advisory board that meets quarterly to review governance matters and reports summarized findings to residents.
Create a digital portal where residents can submit questions and receive automated responses based on pre‑approved policy templates.
review: Balancing openness against security; the options ignore potential overload of information leading to confusion.

name: Vertical Waste Management Protocol
lever_classification: operations — how waste is processed and disposed within the silo
consequences: Pulling this lever changes waste handling, reduces landfill need, but increases energy consumption for incineration.
options: Implement a centralized waste transfer system that routes all refuse to a single underground incinerator, reducing handling steps.
Adopt a modular waste segregation system that separates recyclables at source, enabling on-site material recovery.
Deploy autonomous waste collection drones that transport refuse to designated processing zones, minimizing human exposure.
review: Balancing waste volume reduction with energy use, but options overlook the thermal load on the air filtration system during incineration.

name: Subterranean Energy Storage Strategy
lever_classification: product — how energy is stored and managed underground
consequences: Pulling this lever changes energy resilience, but adds storage footprint and maintenance complexity.
options: Install large-scale underground lithium-ion battery banks adjacent to the main power plant, providing rapid discharge during peak demand.
Construct compressed-air energy storage caverns that absorb excess generation and release pressure during outages in the grid.
Deploy flywheel energy storage units within the central atrium, converting kinetic energy into electrical output.
review: Enhancing grid stability through storage, yet options ignore the seismic risk to underground caverns during seismic events.

name: Population Mobility Regulation
lever_classification: governance — how residents move within the silo
consequences: Pulling this lever controls crowd flow, improves safety, but may restrict emergency evacuation speed.
options: Enforce a staggered shift schedule that limits the number of residents on each floor during peak hours.
Implement a biometric access system that tracks individual movement and enforces designated travel corridors within the complex.
Introduce a real-time crowd density monitoring platform that dynamically redirects traffic across floors during high-occupancy periods.
review: Managing daily traffic flow to reduce congestion, but options fail to account for sudden mass movement during a crisis.

name: Emergency Evacuation Blueprint
lever_classification: execution — how evacuation is planned and executed
consequences: Pulling this lever ensures rapid egress, but may require costly infrastructure upgrades.
options: Designate multiple redundant vertical escape shafts equipped with fire suppression systems to accommodate simultaneous egress.
Develop a modular evacuation pod system that can be deployed quickly to transport groups to safe zones.
Establish an automated alert network that triggers floor-level evacuation protocols for each floor based on hazard detection.
review: Preparing for rapid evacuation, yet options overlook the logistical challenge of coordinating pod deployment across 144 floors.

name: Information Flow Control
lever_classification: dissemination — how information about the outside world is filtered
consequences: Pulling this lever shapes perception, but may create information fatigue among residents.
options: Implement a scheduled bulletin system that releases curated news updates at fixed intervals, limiting spontaneous exposure.
Deploy a digital gatekeeper that screens all incoming media through AI filters before distribution to residents.
Create a community discussion forum moderated by trained facilitators to guide collective interpretation of external events.
review: Regulating external information to maintain order, but options ignore the psychological impact of prolonged information restriction.

name: Maintenance Scheduling Cadence
lever_classification: operations — how routine maintenance is timed
consequences: Pulling this lever optimizes system uptime, but may increase worker fatigue due to shift changes.
options: Adopt a rolling maintenance schedule that rotates teams across floors, ensuring continuous coverage without long idle periods.
Implement predictive maintenance algorithms that trigger repairs only when sensor data indicates imminent failure in the system.
Deploy autonomous maintenance robots that perform routine inspections during off-peak hours, reducing human labor for critical systems.
review: Improving system reliability through scheduled upkeep, yet options miss the cumulative wear on shared infrastructure during overlapping maintenance windows.
